dialogues: You just wrote the same fact two ways. Now I'm going to show you a different rectangle and a different fact. Show me you can do the same thing, two ways around, with a fact you haven't seen yet today. Three quick questions. Take your time.
Question 1. Look at this rectangle: 5 times 8 equals 40.
Right. 5 times 8 equals 40 AND 40 equals 5 times 8. The equal sign means both sides have the same value.
That's it. 7 times 6 equals 42 AND 42 equals 7 times 6. Both orientations name the same rectangle.
You've got it. 9 times 4 equals 36 AND 36 equals 9 times 4. You can write the same fact both ways around.
